source: Special Olympics Football Coaching Guide (2004) + Zimbabwe Grassroots Football Development Framework

warm_up.description: Every training session and match must begin with a proper warm-up. This prepares the body for exercise and reduces injury risk.

components.name: Aerobic Run
components.instructions: Easy jogging to gradually raise heart rate and body temperature. Players jog around the field at a comfortable, conversational pace. No sprinting.
coaching_points: Relaxed, upright posture
Arms swinging naturally
Breathing comfortably — can hold conversation
Do not rush this phase

components.name: Stretching
components.instructions: Full-body stretch sequence. Hold each stretch for 15–30 seconds. Do not bounce. Stretch to the point of gentle tension, not pain.
sequence: Deep hip stretch / hip flexor stretch
Hip rolls (hands on hips, circular motion)
Calf stretch (wall lean, heel on ground)
Hurdle stretch (one leg extended, lean to foot)
Quad stretch (standing, pull foot to glutes)

components.name: Rhythm Drills (Plyometrics — Light)
components.instructions: Dynamic movements to activate fast-twitch muscles and neuromuscular coordination. Perform in a straight line across the field and back.

drills.name: Easy Skipping
drills.description: Skip lightly with arms swinging, focus on rhythm and coordination

drills.name: High Skipping
drills.description: Exaggerate the skip — drive knee up high, push off toes for maximum height

drills.name: Butt Kicks
drills.description: Jog forward kicking heels up to touch buttocks. Fast, rhythmic movement.

drills.name: High Knees
drills.description: Drive knees up to hip height with each step. Pump arms aggressively.

drills.name: Skipping Kicks
drills.description: Skip forward, kicking one leg out straight in front (alternating). Stretches hamstrings dynamically.

coaching_points: Begin slowly and build speed through each drill
Focus on correct form before speed
Arms should mirror leg movement
Light contact with the ground — stay on toes

cool_down.description: Every session must end with a proper cool-down to return the body to its resting state, remove waste products from muscles and reduce next-day soreness.

components.name: Slow Aerobic Run
components.instructions: Easy jog, gradually slowing to a walk over the 5 minutes. This helps flush lactic acid from muscles.

components.name: Light Stretching
components.instructions: Repeat key stretches from warm-up but hold longer (30 seconds each). Focus on muscles used most during the session.

coaching_points: Do not let players leave immediately after a session — cool-down is mandatory
Use this time for a team debrief or coaching talk
Hydrate during cool-down

stretching_catalog.description: Complete stretching library. All stretches should be held for 15–30 seconds. Do not bounce. Breathe steadily throughout.

stretches.name: Chest Opener
stretches.instructions: Stand upright, extend both arms wide to the sides, palms forward. Take a deep breath in, expanding the chest. Hold. Exhale slowly.

stretches.name: Side Stretch
stretches.instructions: Stand feet shoulder-width apart. Raise one arm overhead and lean to the opposite side. Keep hips square. Repeat both sides.

stretches.name: Triceps Stretch
stretches.instructions: Raise one arm overhead, bend elbow so hand drops behind head. Use other hand to gently push elbow back. Repeat both sides.

stretches.name: Shoulder Cross-Body Stretch
stretches.instructions: Bring one arm across the chest at shoulder height. Use the opposite forearm to press it gently closer to the body. Repeat both sides.

stretches.name: Deep Hip Stretch (Hip Flexor Lunge)
stretches.instructions: Step forward into a lunge position. Lower the back knee toward the ground. Push hips gently forward until you feel a stretch in the front of the back hip. Keep torso upright. Repeat both sides.

stretches.name: Groin Stretch (Butterfly / Seated)
stretches.instructions: Sit on the ground. Bring the soles of both feet together in front of you. Let knees fall outward. Hold ankles and gently press knees toward the ground. Sit tall.

stretches.name: Hip Rolls
stretches.instructions: Stand feet shoulder-width apart, hands on hips. Rotate hips in large slow circles — 5 times clockwise, 5 times counter-clockwise.

stretches.name: Calf Stretch
stretches.instructions: Stand facing a wall. Place hands on wall. Step one foot back, keeping heel flat on ground, leg straight. Lean forward until stretch is felt in calf. For deeper stretch, slightly bend the back knee (targets soleus). Repeat both sides.

stretches.name: Hamstring Stretch (Seated)
stretches.instructions: Sit on the ground with both legs extended straight in front. Keeping back flat (not rounded), reach both hands forward toward toes. Hold at point of tension.

stretches.name: Seated Straddle Stretch
stretches.instructions: Sit with legs spread wide apart (straddle position). Keeping back flat, reach both hands forward along the floor between your legs. Then reach to each foot alternately.

stretches.name: Hurdle Stretch
stretches.instructions: Sit with one leg extended straight, other leg bent with foot near opposite thigh (like a hurdler position). Reach forward toward the straight leg's foot. Repeat both sides.

stretches.name: Quad Stretch (Standing)
stretches.instructions: Stand on one foot (hold wall for balance if needed). Bend the other knee, pulling the foot toward the buttocks. Hold ankle — not the shoe. Keep knees together. Repeat both sides.

plyometrics.description: Plyometric exercises develop power, explosiveness and neuromuscular coordination. Begin with rhythm drills and only advance to power exercises once rhythm drills are mastered. Volume and intensity must remain low-to-moderate for youth players.

age_guidance.foundation_u10: Rhythm drills only — no power plyometrics
age_guidance.development_10_15: Rhythm drills + basic power exercises at low volume
age_guidance.performance_15_18: Full plyometric program at moderate volume
age_guidance.professional: Full program, periodized — reduce to one light session per week during peak competition. Stop strenuous plyometrics 7–10 days before playoffs.

rhythm_drills.description: Light, rhythmic movements that improve coordination, foot speed and neuromuscular activation. Always performed first.

exercises.name: Easy Skipping
exercises.instructions: Skip lightly with a natural arm swing. Focus on rhythm and relaxation. Distance: one width of the field and back.

exercises.name: High Skipping
exercises.instructions: Exaggerate the skip by driving the lead knee up as high as possible on each step. Push forcefully off the toes. Arms drive upward.

exercises.name: Butt Kicks
exercises.instructions: Jog forward, kicking heels up rapidly to touch the buttocks on each stride. Keep torso upright and drive arms. Focus on speed of heel recovery.

exercises.name: High Knees
exercises.instructions: Drive knees up to hip height with each step. Arms pump hard. Land on balls of feet. Maintain fast cadence.

exercises.name: Skipping Kicks (Straight-Leg Skip)
exercises.instructions: Skip forward, kicking one straight leg out in front on each skip (alternating). Reach opposite hand toward foot. Dynamically stretches hamstrings.

power_exercises.description: Higher-intensity plyometric exercises for Performance and Professional phase players only. Only introduce once rhythm drills are fully mastered.

exercises.name: Jump Squats
exercises.instructions: Squat to 90 degrees, then explode upward, leaving the ground. Land softly on balls of feet, immediately descend into next squat.

exercises.name: Bounding
exercises.instructions: Exaggerated running strides with maximum push-off on each step. Drive knee up, reach for maximum distance per stride. Land and immediately bound again.

exercises.name: Lateral Bounds
exercises.instructions: Push off one foot to bound sideways to the other foot. Stick the landing briefly before bounding back. Develops lateral power for cutting and defending.

aerobic_fitness.description: Aerobic (endurance) fitness allows players to maintain performance throughout a 90-minute match. Developed through sustained moderate-intensity running.

target_by_phase.foundation: Not required — game activity is sufficient
target_by_phase.performance: 30–45 minute runs at 65–75% max heart rate
target_by_phase.professional: Periodized aerobic base work — volume varies with season phase

methods.name: Steady Pace Run
methods.description: Continuous running at a comfortable, sustainable pace. The player can speak in short sentences but not hold a full conversation.
coaching_points: Upright posture, relaxed shoulders
Arms at 90 degrees, driving forward and back
Land mid-foot, not heel
Breathing controlled — in through nose, out through mouth where possible
Do not let players walk — maintain steady jog throughout
methods.progression: Begin with 20 minutes. Add 5 minutes each week until reaching 30–45 minutes.

anaerobic_fitness.description: Anaerobic fitness allows players to sprint, recover and sprint again. Football is an intermittent sport — players sprint, jog, walk and sprint again throughout a match.

target_by_phase.foundation: Not required — game activity is sufficient
target_by_phase.development: Beginning to develop — short shuttle runs 1x per week
target_by_phase.performance: Fartlek runs and shuttle runs 2x per week
target_by_phase.professional: Sprint intervals, fartlek and shuttle runs — periodized with season

methods.name: Fartlek Run
methods.description: Fartlek means 'speed play' in Swedish. The player alternates between jogging and sprinting at irregular intervals. Simulates the demands of a football match.
protocol: Jog one straight of the field (relaxed pace)
Sprint one straight of the field (full pace)
Walk around the corners if needed
Repeat for full duration without stopping
Alternatively: sprint to every second lamp post, jog to the next
coaching_points: Encourage maximum effort on sprint sections
Recovery jog should be slow enough to allow breathing to settle
Players should feel challenged but not exhausted
Increase sprint sections relative to jog sections as fitness improves

methods.name: Shuttle Runs
methods.description: Short sharp sprints with direction changes. Develops acceleration, deceleration and agility — all critical in football.
methods.setup: Mark lines at 5, 10, 15, 20 and 25 metres from a start line
protocol: Sprint to 5-metre mark and back to start
Sprint to 10-metre mark and back to start
Sprint to 15-metre mark and back to start
Sprint to 20-metre mark and back to start
Sprint to 25-metre mark and back to start
That is one full shuttle set
coaching_points: Touch the line at each marker before turning
Low body position when turning — bend knees, not waist
Drive arms hard on acceleration
Full sprint effort — not a jog

methods.name: Sprint Intervals
methods.description: Timed sprint repeats over a fixed distance. Develops maximum speed and speed endurance.
coaching_points: Maximum effort on every sprint
Do not rush recovery — walk fully back
Technique must be maintained — not a shuffling jog

strength_training.description: Football-specific strength developed through bodyweight exercises. No weight room until late Performance or Professional phase. Core, legs and upper body all important for football performance.

age_guidance.foundation_u10: No structured strength training — game and play activities sufficient
age_guidance.development_10_15: Bodyweight only — focus on correct form before volume
age_guidance.performance_15_18: Bodyweight circuits. Medicine ball introduced late in this phase.
age_guidance.professional: Full strength program including gym work where available

exercises.name: Push-Ups
instructions: Body in a straight line from head to heels (plank position)
Lower chest to 2 cm from ground
Do not let hips sag or pike
exercises.progression: When player can complete all reps with perfect form, increase reps or add a set

exercises.name: Lunges
instructions: Step one foot forward into a large stride
Lower back knee toward the ground — do not let it touch
Front knee must not pass over the toes
Push off front foot to return to start
exercises.advanced: 4 sets of 20 each leg + walking lunges

exercises.name: Sit-Ups / Crunches
instructions: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat on ground
Hands crossed on chest or fingertips at temples (not pulling neck)
Curl upper body toward knees — shoulder blades leave ground
exercises.variation: Add a twist (oblique crunch) — bring elbow toward opposite knee

exercises.name: Medicine Ball Throws
exercises.target: Core, upper body power, total-body coordination
exercises.equipment: Medicine ball (3–5 kg for youth, 5–8 kg for senior)

variations.name: Overhead Throw
variations.instructions: Stand 2 meters from a wall or partner. Hold ball with both hands overhead. Throw forward and down with full body force. Catch and repeat.

variations.name: Forward Chest Pass Throw
variations.instructions: Hold ball at chest. Push/throw directly forward toward partner or wall with both hands. Catch and repeat.

variations.name: Side Throw (Rotational)
variations.instructions: Stand sideways to wall or partner. Hold ball with both hands at hip. Rotate and throw ball explosively against wall using core rotation. Catch and repeat. Do both sides.

variations.name: Straight Arm Forward Throw
variations.instructions: Hold ball in front at waist height with straight arms. Swing ball upward and release forward at chest height, using momentum from legs and core.

weekly_home_training_programs.description: Structured home training programs for players to follow between team sessions. Minimum requirements by phase.

days.activity: Tap ball with feet alternately for 15 seconds. Count and try to beat personal best each session.
Kick ball at a target — wall, fence or makeshift goal. 10 kicks each session.
Receive passes from a family member. Focus on stopping the ball cleanly.
Dribble around objects at home — chairs, shoes, cones. Both feet.
Play freely with a ball. Enjoy football — no structured drills.
Set a personal best record. Count consecutive touches. Track progress weekly.
20 shots at target — aim for corners. Mix power shots and placed shots.
Two-touch drills with a partner. Receive with one touch, pass back with second touch.
Cone course using both feet. Time yourself and try to improve each session.
20-minute steady jog with a ball — dribble during the jog.
Play street football or organize a mini game with friends.
Push-ups, lunges, sit-ups, medicine ball throws. 3 rounds. Focus on correct technique.
Juggling, dribbling, shooting — 30 minutes of individual technical work.
Fartlek run 20 minutes OR shuttle runs (5/10/15/20/25 metres) x 3 sets.
Passing and control drills with a partner. Vary distances and surfaces.
Mental imagery session — 10 minutes visualizing key skills and match situations. Then rest.
Match or free training based on competition schedule.

programs.note: Players at this level maintain personal training schedules. Recovery and mental preparation are equally important as physical training.

days.activity: Full gym session or comprehensive bodyweight circuit. Compound movements prioritized.
Individual technical work — 45 minutes. Juggling, dribbling patterns, finishing.
Sprint intervals or fartlek run. Maximum intensity. 20–30 minutes.
Watch match footage, study opponent patterns. Follow with 15-minute mental imagery session.
Light jog 15 minutes, full stretching routine, cool down. Active recovery only.
Match day or complete rest depending on competition schedule.

mental_training.description: Mental preparation is a core component of football development, particularly from Development phase onwards.

techniques.name: Mental Imagery (Visualization)
techniques.description: The player mentally rehearses skills, movements or game situations in their mind with vivid detail. Research shows mental practice improves physical performance.
how_to: Find a quiet place and close your eyes
Picture yourself on the football field
Mentally perform the skill step by step — see yourself doing it correctly
Include sensory details: feel the ball, hear the crowd, see teammates
Practice for 10 minutes before sleep or before training
techniques.when_to_use: Before training, before matches, during recovery sessions
skills_to_visualize: Controlling a long ball — feel the ball settle
Goalkeeper saves — diving to the correct corner
Set-piece runs — timing the run, meeting the cross
